title: [Pinsir] Battle Guide
summary: A [moxie] snowball sweeper whose Attack climbs with every KO. One [swords-dance] is usually enough to break an entire bench.
strengths: [moxie] stacks Attack on every KO for uncapped pressure
125 base Attack with a functional 85 Speed tier
[swords-dance] lets it overwhelm neutral walls outright
weaknesses: Thin bulk folds instantly to priority
No pivoting move means bad switch-in timing is punished hard

recommendedMoves.reason: Punishes the [Flying] and [Fire] attackers that try to [revenge] [Pinsir]
One setup turn flips [Pinsir] into a neutral-hit cleaner

recommendedItems.reason: Guarantees the first turn de[spite] the frail bulk
Trades setup for Speed, accelerating the snowball

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Pinsir] is a single-type [Bug] physical attacker whose entire plan is wrapped around [moxie] — every KO it scores raises Attack by a stage, and that snowball is what keeps this old Kanto bruiser relevant in the Champions meta. 125 Attack and 85 Speed aren't top tier on paper, but one [swords-dance] turns [Pinsir] into a neutral-hit juggernaut that breaks most walls in two hits. The role is simple: earn one safe setup turn and ride the [moxie] chain.

## Strengths
The [moxie] plus [swords-dance] stack remains terrifying. A three-attack coverage core of [x-scissor], [close-combat], and [stone-edge] hits [Steel], [Fairy], [Flying], and [Fire] pivots from useful angles, so switch-prediction against [Pinsir] is nearly impossible for the opponent. [Bug] STAB also happens to blast the [Psychic] and [Dark] cores that currently define the metagamePinsir's entry can single-handedly tip tempo in the whole game.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Fire], [Flying], [Rock], and [psychic] all hit for super effective. Worse, [Pinsir] is extremely vulnerable to [extreme-speed] and other priority — 65/100/70 defenses with a thin special side fold to one mistimed Hydro Pump as well. It has no pivot move, so once you commit [Pinsir] to the field you are locked in. You must read the opposing lineup before committing the entry.

## Recommended Moveset
[x-scissor] is the locked STAB because it has no recoil or drop. [close-combat] is mandatory coverage against [Steel] and [Rock] tanks that would otherwise wall the [Bug] STAB. [stone-edge] is the panic button that [revenge]-kills the [Flying] and [Fire] attackers who try to ignore the [Bug] STAB. The last slot should be [swords-dance] — two turns of setup is usually all [Pinsir] needs to close a game. If you prefer extra ground coverage, [earthquake] swaps in for [stone-edge].

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item since [Pinsir]'s bulk is too thin to setup without insurance. [choice-scarf] is the alternate build that trades a dance for raw Speed, letting [moxie] chain off [revenge] kills instead. The ability is always [moxie] — hyper-cutter is too narrow against this coverage pool. Tera [Flying] flips the [Rock], [Fighting], and [Ground] weaknesses into resists and also turns [close-combat] partners into frightening matchups, while Tera [Bug] simply maxes STAB damage on a clean [x-scissor].

## Teammates & Synergy
[Pinsir] wants a teammate that can create free setup turns — status spreaders, screens, or fast [u-turn] pivots all qualify. Intimidate users mitigate priority problems on the back line, and a dedicated answer to [Rock] or [Fire] attackers (bulky Waters or [Ground] types) keeps [Pinsir] from getting [revenge]-killed before it starts its chain. A [prankster] [taunt] user also helps against opposing setup mirrors.

## Play Patterns
The entire [Pinsir] plan is built on the first setup turn. With [focus-sash], dance first, then pick the correct coverage move for the incoming switch. With [choice-scarf], come in to [revenge] a weakened threat and pick [x-scissor] or [close-combat] based on typing. Save the Tera for the exact moment a priority move or a quad weakness would otherwise end the sweep early
